solution.p: We evaluate the expression by remembering that x^{-n} is the same thing as \displaystyle{\frac{1}{x^n}} for any non-zero, real value of x.
The first thing we do is to rewrite the expression using fractions- then we remember that when we divide by a fraction it is the same thing as multiplying by its reciprocal.
Effectively we flip the fraction since both variables are raised to a negative exponent.
